---
title: "Charlotte Champion Pascoe"
---

## Charlotte Champion Pascoe

**Dates:** (1782-20 May 1874)

**Maiden name:** (Willyams)

### Description

writer

### Biography

She was the daughter of James Willyams (1741-1828) and married the Rev. T. Pascoe, Vicar of St. Hilary. She was author with her sister Jane Louisa Willyams (d. unm) of a novel, <i>Coquetry</i> 3 vols (1818), and of <i>Walks about St. Hilary</i> which was published posthumously in 1879 edited by C.G.B. Rogers (her first cousin) and Mary Rogers, perhaps niece of the latter's husband.  Administration of her will was granted to her nephew Humphry Grylls.  Walter Scott had helped with the publication of <i>Coquetry</i>.  [will; LG 1906 Willyams, Rogers of Penrose;  <i>Bibliotheca Cornubienses</i>, has b.1781?]

### Letters

- Letter from Oct 22nd [1879?]
- Letter from Aug 31st 1860
- Letter from Easter Eve [<i>c</i>. 1853-1862]

---

[← Back to all letters](../letters.html)
